definition:
1. a stem, tree , (Odyssey by Homer):—;commonly a plank  or beam , (Homer); δόρυ νήϊον a ship's plank , (Homer):—; hence, 
2. a ship  is called δόρυ, like Lat. trabs, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
3. the shaft of a spear , (Iliad by Homer): then, generally, a spear, pike , (Homer), etc.; εἰς δόρυ ἀφικνεῖσθαι to come within a spear's  throw, (Xenophon Historicus); ἐπὶ δόρυ to the spear-side , i. e. the right hand , opposite to ἐπ᾽ ἀσπίδα, (Xenophon Historicus):—;also, the pole  of a standard, (Xenophon Historicus) 
4. metaphorically, δουρὶ κτεατίζειν to win wealth by the spear , il.; δορὶ ἑλεῖν (Thucydides); in [variant datesTragica Adespota to express an armed force.  (ML)
